items.title: Drone kills 2 in Kherson minibus strike, as Russia claims front-line progress in Ukraine
items.summary_auto: Two people were killed after a Russian drone attack on a minibus in the southern Ukrainian city of Kherson, local officials said Saturday, in the latest barrage of civilian areas, a hallmark of Moscow's full-scale invasion of its neighbour.

items.title: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon kill at least 7 despite ceasefire deal
items.summary_auto: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed at least seven people and wounded others on Saturday despite a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, officials said.

items.title: U.S. warns shipping firms could face sanctions over paying Iran's Strait of Hormuz tolls
items.summary_auto: Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz to normal traffic by attacking and threatening to attack ships after the U.S. and Israel launched a war on Feb. 28.

items.title: Spirit Airlines shuts down, blames cost of fuel due to Middle East war
Who won the lottery? Why you may never know the full name of some jackpot winners going forward
A destructive, invasive insect has turned up in Ontario. In the U.S., people are told to stomp it
items.summary_auto: A recent spotted lanternfly discovery in St. Catharines, Ont., is renewing concern about the destructive insect reaching Canada from the United States. Experts say the invasive insect poses a serious threat to vineyards, fruit trees and hardwoods because it feeds on plant sap and can kill grapevines.

items.title: Why saunas are becoming a hot new place to party
items.summary_auto: Picture this: a DJ, a makeshift dance floor and drinks. Only it’s 8 p.m. on a Wednesday and there’s no alcohol — instead, there's a sauna.

items.title: 'He was the first one to call me mom,' mother says of teen killed in Hamilton mall shooting
items.summary_auto: Nabil Askafe's mother and father sat down for an interview with CBC Hamilton this week, days after the 16-year-old was shot and killed at Jackson Square mall in Hamilton.

items.title: This map tracks some of the strangest quirks in Ontario's highway network
items.summary_auto: Jonathan Silverman, a first year civil engineering student at the University of Waterloo, has created an Ontario Oddities map that highlights weird things people might see on a road trip, including signs that are wrong, abandoned bridges and roadways or even submerged highways.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Toronto's High Park cherry blossoms in peak bloom
items.summary_auto: Every spring, tens of thousands of people flock to High Park in Toronto to catch a glimpse of one of the city’s most fleeting spectacles: the cherry blossom bloom.

items.title: Iranian soccer official says he's talking to authorities after former IRGC commander denied entry to Canada
items.summary_auto: A senior Iranian soccer official says he’s been in contact with "relevant authorities" after Canada’s immigration minister confirmed the government revoked travel documents granted to  a former commander of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps to attend a FIFA meeting in Vancouver.

items.title: Online harassment against female journalists is a growing problem, UN report suggests
items.summary_auto: A new UN report released Thursday puts numbers to what many female journalists already know: Online violence against women in the media is growing, becoming increasingly sophisticated, and is affecting female journalists both professionally and personally.

items.title: Canadian sprinters qualify 4 relay teams for 2027 world championships
items.summary_auto: The Canadian 4x100-metre mixed relay team of Eliezer Adjibi, Marie-Éloise Leclair, Duan Asemota, and Audrey Leduc broke a world record at the World Athletics Relays on Saturday in Gaborone, Botswana.

items.title: Ontario map highlights highway oddities from submerged roads to abandoned bridges
items.summary_auto: Jonathan Silverman, a first year civil engineering student at the University of Waterloo, has created an Ontario Oddities map that highlights weird things people might see on a road trip, including signs that are wrong, abandoned bridges and roadways or even submerged highways.

items.title: Why the families of Tumbler Ridge shooting victims may face 'difficult' issues with OpenAI lawsuits
items.summary_auto: The families of victims of the Tumbler Ridge, B.C., school shooting who are suing OpenAI could face some significant legal hurdles in their attempt to hold the artificial intelligence company partially responsible for the attack.

items.title: Invasive spotted lanternfly found in Ontario. U.S. officials say stomp it
items.summary_auto: A recent spotted lanternfly discovery in St. Catharines, Ont., is renewing concern about the destructive insect reaching Canada from the United States. Experts say the invasive insect poses a serious threat to vineyards, fruit trees and hardwoods because it feeds on plant sap and can kill grapevines.

items.title: Toronto Raptors force Game 7 after OT win against Cavaliers
items.summary_auto: RJ Barrett hit a late, high-bouncing game winner in overtime as the Toronto Raptors extended their season with a 112-110 win over the Cleveland Cavaliers in Friday's Game 6 at Scotiabank Arena.

items.title: A robot fried this rice? B.C. restaurant embraces AI-powered help in the kitchen
items.summary_auto: Royal Garden, Vernon's oldest Chinese restaurant, has just installed a pair of AI-powered machines that will be tasked with preparing stir fries, chow mein and chop suey dishes in just a couple of minutes.

items.title: U.S. withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany, Pentagon announces
Trump signs executive order to broaden sanctions against Cuban government
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Friday ‌broadening U.S. sanctions against the Cuban government, two White House officials told Reuters, as he seeks to put more pressure on Havana after ousting Venezuela's leader.

items.title: Deep snow triggers multiple rescues in Banff National Park
items.summary_auto: Parks Canada has carried out three rescues in Banff National Park in recent weeks, all linked to deep, lingering snow that’s obscuring trails and slowing hikers. Officials warn conditions will persist through May and are urging people to check the Parks Canada website for trail reports before heading out for a hike.

items.title: Spotify's new 'Verified' badge confirms musicians like Taylor Swift, Drake aren't AI
items.summary_auto: "New Music Friday" might look a little different when you open your Spotify app. The world's largest music streaming service has rolled out a new "Verified by Spotify" badge, in a bid to show which artists aren't AI.

items.title: Health Canada approves 2nd generic version of Ozempic — and it's Canadian
items.summary_auto: Health Canada has approved the second generic version of brand-name Ozempic, this one manufactured by Canadian company Apotex.

items.title: Trump tells U.S. Congress that ceasefire 'terminated' Iran conflict, as war powers deadline arrives
items.summary_auto: In a letter to congressional leaders on Friday, U.S. President Donald Trump declared that a ceasefire had "terminated" hostilities against Iran, as he sought to bolster his argument that he does not need lawmakers' permission to continue the conflict.

items.title: How a Crown corporation plans to buy out property owners for its high-speed rail plans
items.summary_auto: The Crown corporation behind Canada’s proposed high-speed rail line has released new details on how it plans to expropriate and compensate land owners to carry out the multibillion-dollar project.

items.title: Missing Oscar found after director forced to check it on a flight out of JFK
items.summary_auto: After being forced to check his Academy Award on a trans-Atlantic flight, recent winner Pavel Talankin's Oscar went missing before an airline tracked it down two days later.

items.title: Air force major and decorated marksman pleads guilty to illegally importing firearms
items.summary_auto: An air force officer and decorated sharpshooter has pleaded guilty to illegally importing eight firearms after gun parts — some without serial numbers — were found stashed among his clothes and shoes when he moved back to Canada.
